title: MCAD: Guide to the MCAD Case Process
description: MCAD guide describing the lifecycle of a discrimination complaint from filing to public hearing: complaint authorization and service, position-statement/rebuttal exchange, investigative conferences, early mediation, probable-cause determination, appeals, mandatory conciliation, discovery and certification of issues, pre-hearing, and the public hearing (M.G.L. c.151B §5, c.30A, 804 CMR 1.12). Representation is not required; no cost to file.
notes: Appeal of an investigative disposition (LOPC/LOJ) must be filed within 10 days. Conciliation is mandatory after a probable-cause finding (c.151B). Initial 300-day complaint deadline is in sub-02-mcad-deadline.
